narration: Define two integer variables: userScore and cpuScore, both initialized to 0.
Create an infinite while loop using 'while true' to handle game rounds.
Print a prompt asking the user to enter 1 for rock, 2 for paper, and 3 for scissors.
Import the java.util.Scanner class.
Create a Scanner object named 'scan' connected to System.in.
Create an integer variable 'userChoice' and assign it the value of scan.nextInt().
Import the java.util.Random class.
Generate a random integer between 1 and 3 using rand.nextInt(3) + 1 and store it in 'cpuChoice'.
Use a series of if-else if statements to determine the winner of each round.
Check if cpuChoice equals userChoice to handle tie scenarios.
If it is a tie, print a message stating 'tie, no points will be awarded'.
Use else-if statements to check if userChoice is 1 (rock) or 2 (paper).
If userChoice is 1, check if cpuChoice is 2 (paper) or 3 (scissors) to determine if the user lost or won.
If userChoice is 1 and cpuChoice is 2, print 'cpu chose paper and you have lost this round' and increment cpuScore.
Define two integer variables: userScore and cpuScore, both initialized to 0.
Create an infinite while loop using 'while true' to handle game rounds.
Print a prompt asking the user to enter 1 for rock, 2 for paper, and 3 for scissors.
Import the java.util.Scanner class.
Create a Scanner object named 'scan' connected to System.in.
Create an integer variable 'userChoice' and assign it the value of scan.nextInt().
Import the java.util.Random class.
Create a Random object named 'rand' with no parameters.
Generate a random integer between 1 and 3 using rand.nextInt(3) + 1 and store it in 'cpuChoice'.
Use a series of if-else if statements to determine the winner of each round.
Check if cpuChoice equals userChoice to handle tie scenarios.
If it is a tie, print a message stating 'tie, no points will be awarded'.
Use else-if statements to check if userChoice is 1 (rock) or 2 (paper).
If userChoice is 1, check if cpuChoice is 2 (paper) or 3 (scissors) to determine if the user lost or won.
If userChoice is 1 and cpuChoice is 2, print 'cpu chose paper and you have lost this round' and increment cpuScore.
If userChoice is 1 and cpuChoice is 3, print 'cpu chose scissors and you have won this round' and increment userScore.
If userChoice is 2, check if cpuChoice is 1 (rock) or 3 (scissors) to determine if the user lost or won.
If userChoice is 2 and cpuChoice is 1, print 'cpu chose rock and you have won this round' and increment userScore.
If userChoice is 2 and cpuChoice is 3, print 'cpu chose scissors and you have lost this round' and increment cpuScore.
If userChoice is 3, check if cpuChoice is 1 (rock) or 2 (paper) to determine if the user lost or won.
If userChoice is 3 and cpuChoice is 1, print 'cpu chose rock and you have lost this round' and increment cpuScore.
If userChoice is 3 and cpuChoice is 2, print 'cpu chose paper and you have won this round' and increment userScore.
Check if cpuScore or userScore equals 2 to determine the game winner.
If cpuScore equals 2, print 'sorry but you have lost best two out of three' and break the loop.
If userScore equals 2, print 'congrats you have won best two out of three' and break the loop.
